what_it_does: Standalone integration smoke test that requires a live Ollama service, calls a real model through the fabric client with a Pydantic schema, runs a two-node constant->llm graph via the scheduler, and verifies that a second run memoizes the LLM call instead of re-hitting the model.
observations: Requires the Ollama service to be reachable and fails loudly if not
Uses urllib to probe /v1/models before running
Defines a Pydantic Answer schema and calls client.complete with json=True
Builds a Graph with a constant prompt node feeding an llm node
Runs the graph via scheduler.run and inspects the store head for output
Re-runs the same graph to assert the LLM node is skipped due to memoization
Uses a global mutable ok flag aggregated by check()
Chooses TEST_MODEL from fcfg.DEFAULT_BRAIN, then COMPANY_BRAIN env, then a hardcoded default
Cleans the store path at test start but leaves it after
Prints PASS/FAIL lines and a final emoji status
standouts: Refuses to skip when the service is down — explicit loud failure
Tests memoization behavior by running the same graph twice
Validates structured JSON output from a live model call
conventions: Standalone __main__ script rather than a pytest test
Health-check gate before expensive live calls
Explicit model resolution with fallback chain
concerns: Global mutable ok state makes the test harder to reason about
Comment says it prefers COMPANY_BRAIN but the code prefers fcfg.DEFAULT_BRAIN over the environment variable
ollama_up builds the probe URL by string replacement, which assumes OLLAMA_DIRECT ends with /v1
Broad exception catch in ollama_up hides the exact failure reason
notes: A loud-fail, live-model integration acceptance test for the fabric, scheduler, and node memoization.
questions: What does E3 refer to in this context?
How is the memo/cache key computed for scheduler.run?
What exact guard logic does fabric.client.complete apply when schema is provided?
What do the constant and llm node implementations look like?
What is fcfg.DEFAULT_BRAIN and how is it configured?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it performs a live model call, validates structured output, runs an AI node end-to-end, and checks memoization.
intention_signals: Demonstrate that the guarded fabric and scheduler can really invoke a live model, enforce structured output, execute a simple AI node graph, and avoid redundant model calls through memoization.
novelty: An integration test that explicitly refuses to run without a live backend and uses a second graph run to prove memoization rather than mocking the model.
contribution: A runnable acceptance check that exercises real model connectivity, structured-response guarding, scheduler-driven node execution, and memoization behavior in one script.
summary_for_embedding: Standalone integration smoke test that calls a live LLM through the fabric, validates a Pydantic-structured response, runs an AI node graph via the scheduler, and asserts memoized re-runs skip the model.
intention_for_embedding: Provide a loud-fail E3 acceptance test proving real model connectivity, guarded structured output, end-to-end node execution, and memoization.

symbols.does: Prints a PASS/FAIL line for a labeled condition and updates the global ok flag.
symbols.description: A tiny assertion helper used by the script; it converts the condition to bool, prints a formatted result, and ANDs it into the module-level ok variable so main can return an overall exit code.

symbols.does: Probes the configured Ollama service by requesting /v1/models with a short timeout.
symbols.description: Returns True if the Ollama HTTP endpoint is reachable, otherwise catches any exception and returns False; used as a gate before running the live integration checks.

symbols.does: Pydantic model defining the expected structured JSON shape for the LLM response.
symbols.description: A simple BaseModel with a single string field capital; passed to client.complete as the schema for a guarded, JSON-mode structured call.

symbols.does: Orchestrates the live integration checks and returns an overall pass/fail exit code.
symbols.description: Verifies Ollama is up, performs a structured model call and checks for Paris, runs a constant->llm graph through the scheduler and checks for non-empty output, then re-runs the graph to verify memoization, finally printing a summary and returning 0 or 1.
